economic_notes: First Nation is a shareholder in Noront Resources Ltd. (150,000 common shares, market value $163,500) as part of MOU for Eagle's Nest nickel-copper-palladium deposit in the Ring of Fire. Post year-end, Wyloo Metals acquired Noront at $1.10/share and First Nation sold shares for $165,000. Participates in Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (OFNLP) distributions of $420,110. Investments in Landmark Inn Limited Partnership (11%), Agoke Development LP (33%), Ginoogam Development Corporation (33%), Kenogamisis Energy LP (25%), Minodahmun Development LP (33%).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) unable to verify investments in various entities, and (2) no consolidated budget prepared so budgeted information not disclosed. Fiscal year is 2022, not 2024 as template default. Five subsidiaries all inactive: 1648973 Ontario Inc., 1670719 Ontario Inc., 1796651 Ontario Inc., Aroland Education Authority, Aroland Industrial LP. No schedule of remuneration provided. Housing units: 7 units secured under CMHC Loan #1 and 8 units under CMHC Loan #2 = 15 total. Obligation under capital lease of $135,449 (school buses) not included in long_term_debt figure. Annual debt service reflects long-term debt repayments only ($254,486); capital lease repayments were an additional $75,240. Deferred revenue is very large at $7.76M relative to total revenue of $12.6M, suggesting significant unspent transfer funds. OFNLP reserve internally restricted at $367,896.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
